notes: New eval: long-lines-and-expressions — tests that the agent keeps (eval(...)) expressions on single lines while actively splitting long function strings using comma-separated quoted strings.
with_skill: 12/12 pass. Correctly splits function string as comma-separated quoted strings. All eval expressions on single lines. No adams_id. Full simulation command.

without_skill: 7/12 pass. Critical failure: breaks FUNCTION= string with & inside double-quoted expression (the exact incident bug). Also adds adams_id everywhere, uses wrong force command (missing 'direct'), and omits simulation parameters.

expectations.text: Script creates a model named 'crane' with three rigid body parts (tower, boom, payload)
expectations.evidence: model create model_name = crane, then part create for .crane.tower, .crane.boom, .crane.payload

expectations.text: Boom markers use eval() with loc_global() to compute positions relative to the boom's CM
expectations.evidence: mkr_boom_base and mkr_boom_tip both use (eval(loc_global(...)))

expectations.text: mkr_boom_tip uses ori_along_axis() for orientation toward the payload
expectations.evidence: orientation = (eval(ori_along_axis(.crane.boom.cm, .crane.payload.cm, 'z')))

expectations.text: Every eval expression (eval(...)) stays entirely on one line - no & inside parentheses
expectations.evidence: All three eval expressions are on single lines with no & continuation inside. Script even cites Rule 16.

expectations.text: The long FUNCTION= expression is split across multiple lines (comma-separated quoted strings), not on one enormous line
expectations.evidence: function = 'STEP5(...)' , & ' + STEP5(...)' , & ' - 2.0 * VZ(...)' — correctly split as comma-separated quoted strings

expectations.text: Two revolute joints (tower-ground, boom-tower) and one spherical joint (payload-boom)
expectations.evidence: Two constraint create joint revolute and one constraint create joint spherical

expectations.text: Force function includes STEP5 ramps and a VZ velocity-damping term
expectations.evidence: STEP5 ramps and - 2.0 * VZ(...) present

expectations.text: Mass/inertia set with part modify rigid_body mass_properties
expectations.evidence: All three parts use part modify rigid_body mass_properties

expectations.text: All object names use full dot-path hierarchy
expectations.evidence: All names use .crane.* paths

expectations.text: No adams_id specified anywhere
expectations.evidence: No adams_id keyword in the script

expectations.text: Cylinder geometry on tower and boom, ellipsoid on payload
expectations.evidence: geometry create shape cylinder for tower and boom; ellipsoid for payload

expectations.text: Uses simulation single_run transient for 3 seconds with 3000 steps
expectations.evidence: simulation single_run transient with all required parameters

expectations.text: Script creates a model named 'crane' with three rigid body parts (tower, boom, payload)
expectations.evidence: model create and three part create present

expectations.text: Boom markers use eval() with loc_global() to compute positions relative to the boom's CM
expectations.evidence: mkr_boom_base and mkr_boom_tip use loc_global

expectations.text: mkr_boom_tip uses ori_along_axis() for orientation toward the payload
expectations.evidence: marker modify with ori_along_axis

expectations.text: Every eval expression (eval(...)) stays entirely on one line - no & inside parentheses
expectations.evidence: orientation = & (newline) (eval(...)) — fragile & between = and the expression

expectations.text: The long FUNCTION= expression is split across multiple lines (comma-separated quoted strings), not on one enormous line
expectations.evidence: & continuation placed INSIDE the double-quoted string — the exact incident bug

expectations.text: Two revolute joints (tower-ground, boom-tower) and one spherical joint (payload-boom)
expectations.evidence: All three joints present

expectations.text: Force function includes STEP5 ramps and a VZ velocity-damping term
expectations.evidence: Uses 'force create single_component_force' (missing 'direct') — will error in Adams

expectations.text: Mass/inertia set with part modify rigid_body mass_properties
expectations.evidence: All three parts use part modify

expectations.text: All object names use full dot-path hierarchy
expectations.evidence: All .crane.* paths

expectations.text: No adams_id specified anywhere
expectations.evidence: adams_id on every part, joint, geometry, and force

expectations.text: Cylinder geometry on tower and boom, ellipsoid on payload
expectations.evidence: Cylinders and ellipsoid present

expectations.text: Uses simulation single_run transient for 3 seconds with 3000 steps
expectations.evidence: Missing type, model_name, initial_static parameters
